executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: stale same-session evidence is being reused as if it were current truth
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: enforce code-level fresh probes for operational queries

evidence_pack.location: turn where the agent answered before using a required tool
evidence_pack.summary: The first reply claimed a system state without any current-turn probe.
Operational queries can complete without hard tool enforcement.

findings.title: Operational answers can bypass real inspection
findings.symptom: The agent gives confident system-state answers before tools run.
findings.user_impact: Users receive wrong operational facts with high confidence.
findings.mechanism: Tool usage is encouraged in prompt text but not always required by code.
findings.root_cause: Prompt-enforced discipline instead of code-enforced discipline.
findings.recommended_fix: Introduce task classification plus mandatory probe execution before final answer generation.

conflict_map.note: Old evidence is accepted as if it were fresh.

contamination_paths.note: A previous observation survives long enough to bias current-turn tool routing.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Force fresh probes for system-state queries
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: It removes the most damaging correctness failure immediately.
